other_observers: none; I believe I was chasing a sighting previously reported on COBIRDS.
description: While scanning Erie Reservoir on January 3, 2010, I noticed a large gull towering over the Ring-billed Gulls that made up the bulk of the birds standing on the ice.  [Photo at http://www.flickr.com/photos/rudi_noodle/4243156220/ - Viewing conditions were heavily overcast and the quality of the photo reflects that.]  The large gull appeared to be about twice the size of the surrounding Ring-billeds. The bill on the large gull was large, thick and completely black. The head was generally white, with a dark shadow surrounding the eye, and a mich light shadow extending behind and above the eye. the back and coverts were dark grey with a checkered black appearance. Breast appeared whitish, with some light streaking toward the flanks. Primary tips on the folded wing appeared completely black with no white "mirrors." Legs were light pink.Behaviors: This particular gull was not active at all during the time I viewed it.Call: did not hearPlumage: 1st winter
